summary:
- Azure Front Door outage on 29 Oct 2025 triggered global service disruptions, with DNS, portal and authentication services affected.  
- Microsoft’s status page and public statements understated the scope, focusing only on the portal while leaving out the underlying DNS/Front Door failure.  
- The incident exposed how many everyday services (Starbucks, Grubhub, Outlook, VS Code, etc.) ultimately depend on a single cloud provider.  
- Commenters noted that even large enterprises lack redundancy or failover plans, and questioned whether the cloud model itself is sustainable.  
- A few users reported that their own migrations away from Azure were already in progress, suggesting the industry is quietly moving away from the big three.
